Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for the PivotPoint Labs company record using crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint" or similar). Expected finding: company_pivot, PivotPoint Labs, 32 employees, Product Design & Development, Austin HQ, contact Sam Delgado (Co-founder & CEO). Read/lookup action.
OE 2: Search CRM for PivotPoint deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_pivot" or dealname: "PivotPoint" or similar). Expected findings: deal_pivot_q2 ($5,803, closedwon, Ines Kovac relocation) and deal_pivot_april_2026_dual ($10,650, inprogress, Nadia Okonjo + Diego Castaneda dual relocation). The April 2026 deal description contains detailed notes on H-1B visa coordination requirements, CNC mill logistics, surcharge breakdown ($3,610 total), and invoice INV-2026-0315. Read/lookup action.
OE 3: Search Airtable for PivotPoint Labs relocation records using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "PivotPoint Labs" or similar) on the Relocations table in base appMoveOpsOps001. Expected findings: Nadia Okonjo (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin, In Progress, San Francisco to Austin, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, move window April 14-18, special requirements include H-1B visa holder requiring coordination with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham at lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com before move, USCIS 10-day address change notification, 5TH-FLOOR VICTORIAN WALKUP with no elevator and narrow stairwells, 4-person crew minimum, L-shaped sectional disassembly required) and Diego Castaneda (recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin, In Progress, Brooklyn to Austin, coordinator Javier Morales, pickup April 12-15 delivery after May 1, special requirements include Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill approximately 600 lbs insured at $42,000 requiring vibration-dampened custom crating and climate-controlled transport 60-80F, 16-day storage gap Brooklyn lease ends April 15 Austin apartment available May 1, storage must have loading dock or ground-floor access, freight elevator available at Brooklyn origin so no walkup issue at pickup). The search will also return Sasha Greenwald (recReloc00000000034, In Progress, New York to Nashville, coordinator Javier Morales, standard package) and Ines Kovac (two records, both Completed, Brooklyn to Austin) - these are not the focus of the current investigation but confirm PivotPoint has additional active and completed relocations beyond the dual move. Read/lookup action.
OE 4: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "walkup" or "extraction" or "piano" or "damaged" or "building assessment" (or similar) to find documentation of the walkup extraction incident on another account. Expected finding: Blessing Okafor's message in channel C006 (#operations), posted April 12, where she admits responsibility for the Emilia Cruz piano damage: "We underestimated the turn radius in that walkup and I green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment when I should have required a pre-move site survey before anyone touched the Steinway." She identifies the vendor as KeyMove. This was on the NorthWind Technologies account (Emilia Cruz, Chicago to Denver relocation). Read/lookup action.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "walkup" or "Emilia Cruz" or "piano" or "KeyMove" or "Craig Nguyen" (or similar) to find the damage documentation from the other walkup incident. Expected finding: Email from Craig Nguyen (craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com) to Blessing Okafor documenting the Steinway Model B grand piano damage during extraction: surface scratch approximately 4.5 to 5 inches on the bass side rim, damage occurred during extraction at the second-floor landing where the turn was tighter than the access assessment indicated. Read/lookup action.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "PivotPoint" or "Sam Delgado" or "Nadia Okonjo" or "Diego Castaneda" or "Lorraine Pham" (or similar) to find client correspondence and attorney coordination status. Expected findings: Sam Delgado's original dual relocation request (pivotpoint_dual_reloc_request) with details on both moves, Sam's follow-up (pivotpoint_dual_reloc_followup) confirming Lorraine Pham is expecting contact from MoveOps team for Nadia's USCIS filing, and Catalina's internal update to David (with CC to Chloe) about the dual relocation including invoice details. Critical discovery: no outgoing email to lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com found anywhere, confirming attorney coordination has NOT started. Read/lookup action.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "PivotPoint" or "Nadia" or "Diego" or "Castaneda" or "Okonjo" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the PivotPoint relocations. Expected finding: absence of any Slack discussion about the Nadia Okonjo or Diego Castaneda dual relocation. PivotPoint Slack mentions are limited to the completed Ines Kovac relocation (Catalina's thank-you note in C002, Hana's NMX-2026-0034 invoice in C005) and the Sasha Greenwald relocation (Catalina's announcement in C002, Hana's INV-2026-0304 in C005). Chloe's ops capacity updates reference PivotPoint only for Kovac and Greenwald. The dual relocation internal update was sent by Catalina as an email to David (CC Chloe), not posted in Slack. This absence reinforces that internal communication gaps exist on these newer moves. Read/lookup action.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for PivotPoint invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to PivotPoint or INV-2026-0315 or similar). Expected finding: Invoice inv_pivotpoint_april_2026_dual, total $10,650, dated April 9 2026 (per Sam's fiscal year request), due May 9 2026, billed to sam@pivotpointlabs.com. Line items include: Standard Relocation Package for Nadia ($3,520), Walkup Surcharge for Nadia ($500), Standard Relocation Package for Diego ($3,520), Custom Crating and Vibration-Dampened Packaging for Diego ($1,200), Climate-Controlled Freight Surcharge for Diego ($850), Interim Storage 16 days for Diego ($640), and Cargo Insurance Rider for Diego ($420). A note embedded in the Cargo Insurance Rider line item (#7, $420) description flags that non-standard line items #4-7 require ops verification from Chloe Vance before finalizing. Read/lookup action.
OE 9: Search Linear for PivotPoint-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "PivotPoint" or project: "proj_pivot" or similar). Expected findings: Existing completed tickets for the Ines Kovac relocation (issue_pivot_kovac_apt, issue_pivot_kovac_flight, issue_pivot_kovac_movers, all state_done). Discovery: no existing Linear tickets for the Nadia Okonjo or Diego Castaneda relocations, confirming gaps in tracking. Read/lookup action.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Sam Delgado using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Sam Delgado" or similar) or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Sam Delgado"). Expected finding: sam@pivotpointlabs.com, Co-founder and CEO, PivotPoint Labs. Read/lookup action.
OE 11: Look up contact details for Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe" or "Chloe Vance" or similar). Expected finding: chloe.vance@moveops.com, Operations Manager. Read/lookup action.
OE 12: Look up Catalina Dubois contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina" or similar). Expected finding: catalina.dubois@moveops.com, Account Manager. Read/lookup action.
OE 13: Create Linear ticket(s) for identified gaps using linear_create_issue on team_relocations (consistent with existing PivotPoint issues under proj_pivot). Tickets should cover: (a) Nadia Okonjo attorney coordination not started -- immigration attorney Lorraine Pham has not been contacted despite Sam confirming she is expecting outreach, USCIS address-change filing must happen before the physical move, this is a compliance blocker, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi, priority 1 (Urgent). (b) Nadia Okonjo walkup site survey required -- 5th-floor Victorian with no elevator and narrow stairwells needs physical pre-move building assessment before movers arrive, referencing the Emilia Cruz walkup extraction failure as a cautionary precedent, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi. (c) Diego Castaneda storage facility verification -- 16-day gap (April 15 to May 1) requires climate-controlled Austin storage with loading dock access for 600-lb CNC mill, no confirmation found that facility is booked, also verify pre-move crating inspection requested for April 10-11, assigned to Javier Morales. Write action.
OE 14: Post a message in channel C006 (#operations) using conversations_add_message to flag the gaps to the ops team. Message should tag Chloe Vance, Fatimah Al-Rashidi, and Javier Morales, detail the specific gaps found on both PivotPoint moves (attorney coordination not started, walkup site survey not done, storage not confirmed, crating inspection unverified), reference the Emilia Cruz walkup incident as the reason site surveys are critical, mention the Linear ticket numbers, and request immediate status updates. Write action.
OE 15: Update the PivotPoint April 2026 dual relocation deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_pivot_april_2026_dual", dealstage: updated stage reflecting current status). The deal is currently "inprogress." The agent should select an appropriate stage reflecting that execution gaps have been identified (the specific target stage depends on available pipeline stages - "inprogress" may remain appropriate if no risk-flagging stage exists, but the stage update call should still be made to fulfill the prompt's "update the deal" request). Write action.
OE 16: Send an email using send_email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com covering both PivotPoint relocations. Content should include: (a) Nadia Okonjo status -- coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi assigned, attorney coordination with Lorraine Pham being initiated, commitment not to schedule the truck until USCIS filing is confirmed, site survey being arranged for the Victorian walkup. (b) Diego Castaneda status -- coordinator Javier Morales assigned, confirming storage facility booking and pre-move crating inspection status, CNC mill handling specifications in place. (c) Invoice INV-2026-0315 ($10,650) confirmation -- dated April 9 per Sam's fiscal year request, surcharge breakdown for transparency. Must not share any internal details about the walkup incident on the other account, internal ticket numbers, or internal process failures. Write action.
OE 17: Schedule a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event with Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com) and Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) as attendees. The meeting should cover review of both PivotPoint relocations including the walkup site survey plan for Nadia, storage and crating verification for Diego, invoice surcharge ops verification, and attorney coordination status. Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
